reviewGoal: Check whether one caller-owned context pack can be reviewed through AIQL using only opaque source handles and bounded claims.

evidenceHandles.label: Evidence A
evidenceHandles.intendedUse: Supports the objective and the claim that the pack stays scoped to one bounded review.
evidenceHandles.caveat: Opaque handle only; the bridge does not prove source freshness, retrieval coverage, or approval.

evidenceHandles.label: Evidence B
evidenceHandles.intendedUse: Supports the claim that the pack keeps copied source truth out of the shared review surface.
evidenceHandles.caveat: The handle cannot prove that every caller redaction choice was sufficient.

evidenceHandles.label: Evidence C
evidenceHandles.intendedUse: Supports the claim that the handle registry is still useful for downstream retrieval.
evidenceHandles.caveat: Retrieval usefulness depends on a caller-owned resolver outside ai-quality-loops.

evidenceHandles.label: Evidence D
evidenceHandles.intendedUse: Supports the weak readiness claim that the pack could drive automated downstream action.
evidenceHandles.caveat: Handle presence alone does not prove approval, routing authority, or remediation readiness.

claims.claim: The pack is scoped to one bounded AIQL review.
claims.caveat: The handle confirms only the caller-written scope statement, not downstream quality.

claims.claim: The pack avoids copying canonical source truth into the shared review surface.
claims.caveat: Callers still own their own redaction and source-handling checks.

claims.claim: The handle registry is useful enough for downstream retrieval without broad rediscovery.
claims.caveat: Usefulness still depends on caller-owned handle resolution and freshness checks.

claims.claim: The pack is ready for automated downstream action.
claims.caveat: This claim is intentionally weak because the bridge omits approval, priority, and routing policy.

constraints: Keep source resolution, freshness checks, public-source selection, approval, routing, and retention caller-owned.
Do not treat handle presence as proof of downstream readiness or action authority.
knownGaps: The bridge omits copied source excerpts, approval state, and action priority by design.
A caller-owned workflow must still verify whether each handle resolves to current source truth before any downstream action.
continuation: Prepare a caller-owned follow-up only when the review finds missing handles, ambiguous evidence coverage, or unsupported readiness claims.
callerOwnedBoundaries: AIQL can review pack quality only; it does not become the source of truth, memory layer, tracker mirror, or routing authority.
Any follow-up prioritization, tracker mutation, or execution decision remains caller-owned outside ai-quality-loops.
reviewerPosture: Report missing evidence handles, ambiguous handle-to-claim coverage, copied-truth risk, and unsupported readiness claims as findings.
Treat the omitted research-source audit and public-source list as boundary reminders, not hidden proof that external checks already passed.
Keep recommendations limited to pack-quality fixes, caller-owned confirmation, and boundary clarification.
